zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jannet Browne於1474年出生在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安時，她的父親George Brown of Coulston36歲，母親Marion Hay31歲。她於1492年與Alexander Cumming of Altyre在英國英格蘭素斯 越可爾威爾斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於1534年在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾斯特拉頓享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Jannet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jannet Browne was born in 1474, in East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, George Brown of Coulston, was 36 and her mother, Marion Hay, was 31. She married Alexander Cumming of Altyre in 1492, in Wales, South Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died in 1534, in Strathdon, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Jannet Browne est née en 1474, à East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, George Brown of Coulston, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Marion Hay, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Alexander Cumming of Altyre en 1492, à Wales, South Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Elle est décédée en 1534, à Strathdon, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jannet Browne(이)가 1474년 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Brown of Coulston(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marion Hay(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1492년 영국 잉글랜드 소우스 요르크실르 월스에서 Alexander Cumming of Altyre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1534년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 스트래스던에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
